MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM videoconference:
Cathryn Fund, Esq., Tel. 818.610.8800, for the Plaintiff;
Dave Wachtel, Esq., Tel. 202.328.1666, for the Defendant.

The parties have previously stipulated to Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein deciding this matter.

Matter called. The Court inquires about the status of the vendor and the search terms.

DISCUSSIONS are held. Mr. Wachtel states that the vendor has been selected, that signatures are needed, and that they sent the search terms to Ms. Fund. Ms. Fund confirms that they are not requesting an expedited search. Mr. Wachtel addresses the cost sharing.

At the conclusion of discussions, Mr. Wachtel is to prepare a final order on DEFENDANT ASIAN PACIFIC AMERICAN LABOR ALLIANCE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions. The Court states that the order should reflect the vendor, the search terms, and the due date/time frame. The proposed order should state that the costs will be shared equally and will cover costs of the prevailing party. Mr. Wachtel is to prepare the final order, submit it to Ms. Fund, and submit it to Judge Pro Tem Stein at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: J. Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Continued To Sep-10-21 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. EDEN KALDERON, Esq. of JML Law (tel: 818-610-8800) for plaintiff;
Atty. DAVID WACHTEL, Esq. of Trister, Ross, Schadler & Gold PLLC (Tel: 202-204-5946) for defendant Asian Pacific American Labor Alliance.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ASIAN PACIFIC AMERICAN LABOR ALLIANCE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued.

The Court continues the matter for a further hearing to occur after the deposition of plaintiff has taken place. The matter is continued for further hearing to September 10, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. The Judge Pro Tem will send a Zoom invitation to the parties for the hearing.

Parties are to file a supplemental brief/status report by August 31, 2021 with a courtesy copy emailed to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Defense counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel for review. The parties are then to submit either an approved proposed order or competing proposed orders in Word format to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
